results.title: Additional Costs Incurred by Government and Public Waste Collectors to Transport, Sort and Incinerate Contaminated Recyclables
results.summary: The Government incurs no additional costs for collecting and incinerating contaminated recyclables from blue bins, as these expenses are covered by fees paid to Public Waste Collectors (PWCs). However, PWCs face variable additional costs for segregation and incineration, which depend on their specific sorting processes and operational efficiency. Contaminated recyclables also impose extra demand on waste management infrastructure and result in lost recycling revenue. Currently, contamination sampling occurs at Materials Recovery Facilities on an aggregated national basis. The National Environment Agency (NEA) will continue exploring methods to enhance data collection for more targeted, community-specific interventions rather than immediately transitioning to estate-level tracking. Residents are urged to deposit only recyclables in blue bins to support sustainability efforts.

results.title: Accommodations for Student-athletes when Trainings and Competitions Clash with Universities' Course Schedules
results.summary: Autonomous universities commit to supporting student-athletes by customizing academic schedules when core module examinations clash with national training or overseas competitions. Arrangements include taking exams before or after events where feasible and considering alternative assessments like online assignments or pre-recorded presentations. These athlete-friendly practices are formalized in a memorandum of understanding signed between the autonomous universities and the Singapore Sports School in April 2025.

results.title: Addressing Challenges and Strengthening English Language Learning Outcomes in Primary Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education employs a balanced pedagogy in primary schools that integrates contextualised communication with explicit grammar and vocabulary instruction. While PIRLS data indicates steady improvement in reading literacy scores over the past two decades, enjoyment of reading among students declined between 2016 and 2021, a trend also observed globally. The Ministry attributes this shift to broader entertainment preferences and plans to partner with schools, public libraries, and parents to sustain reading habits. Broader shifts from reading to other forms of entertainment are identified as challenges to sustaining the reading habit.

results.title: Accounting for Potential Volatility in Global Energy Markets in Singapore’s Energy Transition Strategy
results.summary: Singapore's energy transition strategy addresses global market volatility through diversification across four pillars: natural gas, domestic solar, low-carbon imports, and emerging alternatives. To secure gas supplies, the Government is developing a second liquefied natural gas terminal. Solar deployment targets 2 GWp by 2030, with current capacity at 1.7 GWp as of June 2025, though this is projected to meet only 10% of demand by 2050. The Government supports strategic low-carbon projects via the $10 billion Future Energy Fund and Singapore Energy Interconnections Pte Ltd to mitigate investment risks. Additionally, the Energy Market Authority has awarded approvals for over 8 GW of electricity imports from Malaysia, Thailand, Indonesia, and Australia. The carbon tax remains in place to price emissions, with revenues supporting decarbonisation efforts.

results.title: Addressing Noise Fatigue from Prolonged, Cumulative Exposure and Infrastructure Works and MRT Operations in Yio Chu Kang
results.summary: Noise mitigation measures ensure cumulative noise levels from construction and MRT operations remain within National Environment Agency guidelines for prolonged exposure. Existing barriers undergo regular maintenance, while contractors must conduct localised noise impact assessments before new works, deploying barriers, enclosures, or mufflers as needed. Railway noise is monitored regularly, with additional barriers implemented if guidelines are exceeded. Proactive barrier installation is considered based on resident feedback and population density, though no specific timeline for new infrastructure was stated.

results.title: Adequacy of Grant to Help Smaller Importers in Beverage Container Return Scheme
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the adequacy of the $2,500 grant for smaller beverage importers under the Beverage Container Return Scheme was addressed in a previous response on 3 February 2026. This prior answer covered consultations on compliance costs and the basis for the grant amount, noting that small importers face annual sticker costs exceeding $24,000. The Minister did not provide new details on whether a review of the grant to prevent price increases or reduce compliance burdens is planned.

results.title: Appeals by Foreign Spouses of Singaporeans to Purchase BTO HDB Flats
results.summary: From 2023 to 2025, HDB received 16 appeals from Singaporean couples with non-resident spouses and no Singaporean or SPR child to purchase BTO flats larger than 2-room Flexi units. These appeals were not acceded to because the applicants had alternative housing options. Singaporeans with non-resident spouses who have a Singaporean or SPR child remain eligible to purchase flats of any size subject to standard conditions.

results.title: Assessing Need for Sixth Desalination Plant
results.summary: The Public Utilities Board (PUB) is conducting a feasibility study for a potential sixth desalination plant to address growing water needs and climate risks. Capital and operating costs will be determined after the study concludes, with PUB maintaining a pricing model that reflects the cost of supplying the next drop of water. This approach ensures sustainable financing for long-term investments, safeguarding Singapore's water security without specifying immediate impacts on household or business tariffs.

results.title: Assessment of AI Technology Uptake among Singaporean SMEs Versus MNCs, and Individuals
results.summary: The Government tracks AI adoption through the annual Singapore Digital Economy Report published by the Infocomm Media Development Authority since 2023. This report provides data on AI usage by companies of different sizes, including small and medium enterprises versus multinational corporations, as well as workers in Singapore. The Minister directed the Member to refer to this report for specific breakdowns on adoption rates and active usage statistics across these sectors.

results.title: Average Time Taken from HIP Announcement to Polling of Residents and Start of Construction Works
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the question regarding average HIP timelines was previously addressed in a written reply issued on 3 February 2026. The response covered the average duration from announcement to polling and construction start, major reasons for timeline variations, and measures to compress schedules for better resident planning. No new data or updated statistics were provided in this session. Readers seeking specific figures or current timeline metrics should refer to the original 3 February 2026 reply.

results.title: Balloting and Placement Data for 2025 Secondary School Posting Exercise
results.summary: In the 2025 Secondary 1 Posting Exercise, approximately one in ten students underwent computerised balloting, a rate that has remained stable since 2021. The Ministry of Education does not track the specific number of students unsuccessful in balloting despite meeting cut-off points, as this figure fluctuates annually based on school choices and DSA admissions. While the Minister noted that DSA admissions affect the minimum quota for non-affiliated students, he stated the Ministry will study suggestions regarding hard quotas for non-affiliated and non-DSA admissions. Consequently, no definitive timeline or eligibility rule for such a quota was established during the session.

results.title: Bass Noise Assessment of Shophouse Pubs and Discos to Address Disturbance Reports by Surrounding Units
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) does not regulate nightlife establishments like pubs and discos, which are instead governed by noise control requirements in their Public Entertainment licences issued by the Singapore Police Force (SPF). Upon receiving disturbance reports, NEA will coordinate with SPF and the Urban Redevelopment Authority to engage these establishments in implementing further noise mitigation measures as far as practicable. No specific standards for measuring low-frequency bass sounds were adopted, and no new timeline or operational constraints were stated for addressing such disturbances.

results.title: Breakdown of Day-surgery Bills Fully Covered by MediSave and Bills Requiring Out-of-pocket Payment from 2020 to 2025
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, over 80% of Singapore Citizens did not pay out-of-pocket cash for subsidised day surgeries after MediSave and MediShield Life coverage. Among the minority requiring cash payments, the majority paid less than $500. MediFund remains available to assist those unable to afford remaining healthcare expenses after subsidies and insurance. The Minister confirmed that no citizen will lose access to appropriate healthcare due to an inability to pay. Data for 2025 was not provided in the transcript.

results.title: Breakdown of GCE "N" (Academic) Level and "N" (Technical) Level Results' Grade Distribution for Past Three Years
results.summary: From 2023 to 2025, GCE Normal (Technical) Level students achieved grade D or better in English and Mother Tongue (about 90%) and Mathematics (about 80%). Concurrently, GCE Normal (Academic) Level students secured grade 5 or better in English and Mathematics (over 90%) and Mother Tongue (over 80%). These consistent performance metrics across core subjects for both streams indicate stable academic outcomes without reported declines or specific policy interventions during this period.

results.title: Breakdown of GCE "O" Level Results' Grade Distribution for Past Three Years
results.summary: The Minister for Education reported that the percentage of Singapore-Cambridge GCE "O" Level students achieving grade C6 or better in core subjects (English Language, Mother Tongue Languages, and Mathematics) has remained consistently high from 2023 to 2025. Approximately nine in ten students attained this grade in each subject during this period. The transcript provides this specific statistical breakdown without detailing the exact percentage figures for each individual year.

results.title: Breakdown of Long-term Accident and Health Insurance Premiums Collected and Claims Made by Hospital Plan Type
results.summary: Ministry of Health receives annual submissions from insurers detailing gross premiums and claims for Integrated Shield Plans, disaggregated by ward class and plan type. Data indicates loss ratios are higher for private hospitals compared to public non-subsidised wards. Currently, only aggregate figures are made publicly available. The Minister did not confirm whether insurers will be required to provide this breakdown in future regulatory submissions, nor was a specific timeline for such a change stated.

results.title: Breakdown of PDVL and TDVL First-time Approvals and Renewal Patterns for Past Five Years
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport provided data on first-time Private Hire Car (PDVL) and Taxi Driver's Vocational Licence (TDVL) approvals and renewals for the past five years, along with the distribution of renewal frequencies. He clarified that each licence is valid for three years. The specific numerical breakdowns for approvals, renewals, and renewal counts were presented in the official response. No further details regarding eligibility criteria or policy changes were discussed in this segment of the debate.

results.title: Breakdown of Private-Hire Drivers' Profiles across Gender, Age and Educational Qualifications
results.summary: As of 31 December 2025, the average age of registered private-hire drivers is 52 years. The majority are male with secondary-level education or below, while less than one-third hold tertiary qualifications. The Acting Minister for Transport provided these statistics regarding driver demographics. No further breakdown by specific age groups or educational levels was disclosed in the response.

results.title: Breakdown of Singapore's Foreign Currency Reserves Protected against Exchange Rate Fluctuations and Safeguards to Prevent Excessive Hedging Activity
results.summary: GIC and Temasek manage foreign exchange risk holistically within their mandates, noting that USD hedging costs have risen steadily over the past five years. Specific hedging proportions, internal thresholds, or detailed position disclosures are not provided due to commercial sensitivity. Decisions on risk mitigation strategies are made dynamically by the investment entities based on asset classes and market conditions rather than fixed internal limits. The primary objective remains achieving sustainable long-term returns while considering various risk factors.

results.title: Breakdown on Outcomes of MENDAKI Financial Assistance Applications for Muslims of Non-Malay Descent in Last 10 Years
results.summary: From 2022 to 2025, MENDAKI reported that approximately 70% of non-Malay/Muslim applicants successfully received financial assistance, a rate comparable to that of Malay/Muslim applicants. The Acting Minister clarified that MENDAKI's schemes, including study loans and bursaries, are open to all Malay and Muslim students regardless of their specific ethnic classification on their NRIC. This data indicates that financial barriers to higher education are not disproportionately affecting non-Malay Muslims under the current assistance framework.

results.title: Bus Services Fare Hike at Pathlight School and Analysis of Participants in Tender Exercise
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the circumstances regarding the 40% to 50% fare increase for Pathlight School's bus services, along with the number of bidders and the next-lowest unsuccessful bid quote, were already addressed in the Ministry's reply to Oral Question No 74 on 3 February 2026. No new information or additional details were provided during this session. The specific tender exercise details remain as previously disclosed in the official parliamentary record.

results.title: Cases Registered through Healthcare Mediation Scheme and Mediation Agreements Signed
results.summary: From 2020 to 2025, the Healthcare Mediation Scheme processed 50 cases, resulting in approximately 31 successful mediation agreements signed by consenting parties. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health provided these figures during the parliamentary session. No further details regarding specific case types, average resolution times, or future scheme adjustments were disclosed in the transcript.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Minister of State for Manpower
results.summary: Senior Minister of State for Manpower Dr Koh Poh Koon corrected a previous statement regarding retrenchment notification requirements, clarifying that the threshold applies to companies with 10 or more workers rather than five. Under this rule, any company meeting this size criterion must notify the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) of any retrenchment within five working days, even if only a single employee is affected. This correction updates the factual record from an earlier clarification given during the Parliament Sitting of 4 February 2026, ensuring the eligibility condition for mandatory notification is accurately reflected as a workforce size of 10 or more.

results.title: ComCare Applications Rejected Due to Applicants' Non-compliance with Employment Action Plans
results.summary: The Ministry for Social and Family Development (MSF) does not track the number of ComCare Short-to-Medium Term Assistance (SMTA) applications denied due to non-compliance with employment action plans. While applicants are informed at the outset that adherence to these plans is required, action plans can be reviewed if genuine difficulties arise. In a specific case where an applicant was denied assistance for alleged non-engagement with e2i, the Social Service Office (SSO) is currently considering the appeal. The SSO reviews cessation of financial assistance carefully, involving Social Assistance Officers (SAOs) who interview applicants and consult family coaches and other sources to assess the entire assistance required before making decisions.

results.title: Compliance Rate of Mandatory CPF Contributions and Enforcement Actions Taken against Non-compliance
results.summary: Since the Platform Workers Act commenced in January 2025, the vast majority of platform operators have complied with mandatory CPF contribution requirements. The CPF Board has taken enforcement action against four operators for late or underpaid contributions resulting from administrative lapses, such as incorrect earnings calculations. These cases were resolved within two weeks after reminder notices were issued, with late payment interests charged. To support compliance, the Board issued a guide on good practices and provides platform workers with alerts upon credit of contributions for verification.

results.title: Considerations for Athletes who Peak Later in Sport Excellence Potential and Other Programmes
results.summary: The Acting Minister confirmed that Sport Singapore's High Performance Sport Institute (HPSI) designs support based on individual performance and potential rather than age, with no upper limit on support duration provided athletes meet criteria. The Sport Excellence (spex) framework, including spexPotential, spexScholarship, and spexCarding, offers calibrated support across developmental stages for late bloomers and those switching sports, citing Paralympian Theresa Goh as an example of sustained support from age 14 to 38. While a specific sprinter's recent SEA Games gold medal fell outside standard spexScholarship windows, the Minister noted the athlete was supported via spexCarding and spexGLOW. The Ministry expressed openness to reviewing wider eligibility windows for older athletes with varied life circumstances to better retain talent throughout their careers.

results.title: Consumer Risk Assessment for Overseas Fraud Victims and Regulatory Guidelines for System Alert Delays and Consumer Loss Liability
results.summary: MAS requires banks to provide real-time transaction alerts via SMS, email, or in-app notifications, with major retail banks typically offering parallel notifications through at least two channels. Customers relying solely on SMS are encouraged to opt for additional channels like in-app or email to ensure timely notification even when overseas. When fraud occurs, banks must evaluate each case to determine if notification obligations were met, treating customers fairly under the E-Payments User Protection Guidelines. Unsatisfied victims may approach the Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre for mediation and adjudication regarding loss liability arising from system delays beyond their control.

results.title: Coordination with International Partners to Address Regulatory and Enforcement Gaps in Dealing with Illegal Shipping Practices
results.summary: Singapore addresses illegal shipping practices beyond its territorial waters through international cooperation, specifically via the Malacca Straits Patrol with Indonesia, Malaysia, and Thailand to share information and conduct coordinated patrols. The government exchanges data with partners to investigate breaches of domestic regulations and engages the maritime and financial communities on sanctions and secondary risks. As a Vessel Traffic Service authority, Singapore monitors vessel movements in the Straits of Malacca and Singapore, reporting IMO convention contraventions to Flag States for follow-up. Non-compliant vessels face denial of entry or detention under domestic legislation and IMO instruments, while Singapore fully implements UN Security Council resolutions and does not condone shadow fleet activities.

results.title: Correlation between Higher Phase 2A Take-up Rates in Primary 1 Registration Exercise and Socio-economic Clustering in Schools
results.summary: The Minister for Education acknowledged that Phase 2A alumni priority in Primary 1 registration may contribute to socio-economic clustering in some schools, potentially limiting interaction among students from diverse backgrounds. While most primary schools maintain a good distribution of students, MOE is actively reviewing the Primary 1 Registration Framework to enhance access for students from varied backgrounds. To support social mixing, the Ministry has introduced cluster-based co-curricular activities that bring together students from different schools to pursue common interests. These measures aim to strengthen the foundations of a "we first" society by ensuring children have opportunities to learn with peers from other backgrounds.

results.title: Coverage Selection Criteria for Project Wolbachia in Private Estates and Possible Expansion towards Changi Division
results.summary: Project Wolbachia site selection relies on dengue risk, mosquito population, and estate landscape. Currently, 35% of private landed estate households are included. The National Environment Agency (NEA) plans to expand the project to HDB estates in Pasir Ris, covering parts of Changi Division, starting March 2026. Further expansion sites will be announced by NEA when ready. The Minister confirmed these factors and timelines without introducing new criteria or eligibility rules beyond the stated parameters.

results.title: Criteria to Revise CPF Additional Withdrawal Limits for Integrated Shield Plans
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health explained that MediSave premiums for Integrated Shield Plans (IP) are capped by Additional Withdrawal Limits (AWLs) because IP coverage is optional and unsubsidised. When revising these limits, the government aims to balance enabling Singaporeans to access more private insurance against the need to preserve sufficient MediSave for future basic healthcare needs. Specific criteria for threshold revisions were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Data Collection for Household Sector Balance Sheet at Household and Income Decile Levels
results.summary: The Singapore Department of Statistics compiles the Household Sector Balance Sheet using aggregated data from firms and institutions, aligning with international practice. Consequently, the Ministry will not collect data at the individual household level nor disaggregate the balance sheet by household income deciles. This approach precludes the availability of such granular statistics for analysis.

results.title: Data on "Buy Now, Pay Later" Transactions and Updating Practice Code to Reduce Purchase Limit for Those Below 21
results.summary: The Minister for Finance stated that Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) transactions accounted for less than 2% of total credit and debit card payments in the first half of 2025, with 1.5% of users being under 21 years old. MAS is currently collecting survey data from BNPL firms to monitor sector usage and assess potential concerns, noting that the median outstanding amount for users with missed payments is below $400. While no new measures are deemed necessary at this time, MAS will continue monitoring the situation and reviewing survey enhancements. The existing BNPL Code of Conduct includes safeguards such as suspending facilities upon overdue payments, capping late fees, and prohibiting limits exceeding $2,000 without additional credit assessments.

results.title: Data on Caseloads Handled by Community Outreach Teams and Community Intervention Teams, and Ensuring Professional Credentialing as Teams are Expanded
results.summary: Questions regarding caseload metrics, session frequency, duration, workload sustainability, and professional credentialing for Community Outreach Teams (CREST) and Community Intervention Team (COMIT) workers were raised. The Minister for Health and Coordinating Minister for Social Policies directed that these inquiries be addressed via a written response to Parliamentary Question No 16 at the Parliament Sitting on 3 February 2026. No specific numerical data or operational plans were provided during the oral session.

results.title: Data on Companies with More Than 25 Workers Paying Retrenchment Benefits in Line with Tripartite Guidelines
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that around 90% of eligible employees from companies with 10+ workers received retrenchment benefits between 2020 and 2025, with 80% receiving at least two weeks' salary per year of service per Tripartite Advisory norms. The Government declined to mandate reporting for all retrenchments or impose higher benefit minimums for larger firms, citing the need to balance worker protection with business viability and avoid discouraging long-term hiring. While unionised firms typically pay one month per year of service, the Minister noted flexibility exists for financially distressed companies. No specific date was provided for the last tripartite review of these guidelines, though updates occurred around the COVID-19 crisis.

results.title: Data on DigitalAccess@Home Scheme Household Support and Measures to Increase Take-up Rate
results.summary: Since launching in April 2023, the DigitalAccess@Home Scheme has supported over 30,000 low-income households with subsidised broadband and device packages. The Infocomm Media Development Authority collaborates with the Ministry of Social and Family Development, Ministry of Education, and social sector partners to raise awareness and streamline applications. The Minister clarified that the 60,000 household figure represents resources provisioned to meet demand rather than a fixed target, and the allocated budget of $80 million is used to meet actual qualifying household needs. Promotional efforts will continue to ensure inclusive digital access.

results.title: Data on Distribution of Singaporean Workers in Outward- and Domestic-Oriented Sectors of Years 2015 with 2025
results.summary: In December 2025, outward-oriented sectors employed 1.25 million residents (50.5%), while domestic-oriented sectors employed 1.21 million (48.7%). These figures closely mirror the distribution from ten years prior, with outward-oriented sectors at 50.4% and domestic-oriented sectors at 48.6%. The Minister noted that business transformation and technology adoption will continue to alter job nature, meaning the current employment distribution may evolve. Consequently, the Government will collaborate with tripartite partners to equip Singaporeans with skills required for future roles.

results.title: Data on HDB Household Leaders Aged 65 and Older and Breakdown of Seniors Living in HDB Households from 2018 to 2024
results.summary: Based on the 2023/2024 HDB Sample Household Survey, the estimated number of senior households (where the decision-maker is aged 65+) in sold flats rose from approximately 260,000 in 2018 to 340,000 in 2023/2024. Eligibility requires the decision-maker to be the owner, co-owner, or spouse for sold flats, or the registered tenant or spouse for rental flats. The Minister clarified that HDB does not collect data on the total number of seniors residing within these households, so absolute figures for total seniors remain unavailable.

results.title: Data on Migrant Domestic Workers' Assault Reports to MOM and/or SPF, and Employer's Prosecution and Permanent Bans in Past Five Years
results.summary: From 2021 to September 2025, an average of 300 annual reports of Migrant Domestic Worker abuse were received, with most cases remaining unsubstantiated. Out of 30 prosecuted employers during this period, 14 were convicted and permanently debarred from hiring future domestic workers. Additionally, an average of 70 employers received warnings yearly. All abuse reports are referred to and investigated by the Police.

results.title: Data on Outcome of SpexGLOW Grant Applications and Reasons for Rejections
results.summary: Since 2018, SportSG has supported 216 applications for the spexGLOW Grant for Loss of Wages, which assists TeamSG athletes taking no-pay leave to train and compete. The scheme operates through a collaborative planning process with National Sports Associations to assess eligibility and determine support needs, ensuring qualifying athletes receive assistance. At the 2025 SEA Games, 39 athletes who received spexGLOW support won medals, including Calvin Quek's historic gold in the 400-metre hurdles and Siti Khadijah's bronze in wushu sanda. The Acting Minister noted that the programme specifically targets athletes pursuing full-time training who demonstrate potential or have performed well at major competitions.

results.title: Data on PREDICT Programme Screening and Plans to Expand Outreach of Programme
results.summary: The PREDICT programme, launched in 2024 as a pilot at KK Women's and Children's Hospital, has screened 674 couples by end-2025 for autosomal recessive conditions like Pompe disease and spinal muscular atrophy. The Ministry of Health will assess the pilot's clinical and cost effectiveness upon result availability to determine if the service expands to all obstetric clinics. No specific timeline for expansion or outreach methods has been confirmed yet. The programme currently operates only at KKH while awaiting the final evaluation of its efficacy and economic viability.

results.title: Data on Persons with Autism Spectrum Disorder on Waitlist for Placement at Day Activity Centres
results.summary: There are currently 130 persons with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) waitlisted for placement in the eight specialisation Day Activity Centres (DACs), with an average waiting time of nine months. The Ministry of Social and Family Development plans to increase DAC capacity by 500 places by 2030 to address demand, while also exploring private provider options and alternative service models like the Enabled Living Programme and Enabling Services Hubs. Concerns were raised regarding affordability for households earning above $2,800 and potential underrepresentation of high support needs cases due to long waitlists or cost barriers. The Ministry is conducting a nuanced analysis of island-wide needs and affordability through an inter-agency task force to ensure community-based care remains accessible and sustainable.

results.title: Data on Pet Deaths and Injuries at Pet-related Businesses for Past Three Years
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the Ministry's reply to Ms Jessica Tan on 14 January 2026 addressed the query regarding records of pet deaths or injuries at licensed pet-related businesses. The transcript does not contain the specific figures for the past three years, nor does it provide details on whether such records are maintained. Consequently, the exact data points requested remain untracked in this session.

results.title: Data on Pilgrims Who Have Cleared Medical Screening for Upcoming Haj
results.summary: Approximately 98% of Haj applicants cleared medical screening this year. The Fatwa Committee advises those unable to participate due to health reasons to defer Haj or appoint a proxy for Haj Badal. For individuals with chronic conditions unlikely to recover, the Haj obligation is lifted if a proxy cannot be arranged. MUIS will provide further details on the Haj Badal arrangement when ready.

results.title: Data on Platform Workers CPF Transition Support Scheme Uptake, Disbursement and Rejection
results.summary: As of 2025, approximately 24,000 lower-income platform workers qualified for the Platform Workers CPF Transition Support (PCTS) scheme, with over $4 million disbursed. The Central Provident Fund Board automatically assesses eligibility monthly without requiring applications from workers. Data for January 2026 is not yet available. The transcript does not specify the percentage of rejected applications or the primary reasons for rejections.

results.title: Data on Real-term Growth in Tourism Receipts after Accounting for Inflation
results.summary: Between 2019 and 2024, real-term tourism receipts declined by 4.3% after accounting for inflation. However, spend per visitor rose 10.7% from $1,630 to $1,800 in real terms during the same period. Full-year 2024 receipts reached $29.8 billion, with the first nine months showing a 5.8% real-term growth compared to the prior year. The Singapore Tourism Board continues to pursue high-growth visitor segments and markets with higher spending propensity through the Quality Tourism strategy.

results.title: Data on Rejected Applications for Subsidies under Seniors Mobility and Enabling Fund
results.summary: Under the Seniors Mobility and Enabling Fund, applicants for subsidised Motorised Devices must meet three additional criteria: a doctor's memo confirming the need to age in place, mental and physical fitness to drive, and an inability to use a manual wheelchair independently or have a full-time caregiver. Consequently, some applicants are rejected for not fulfilling these conditions. The Ministry of Health and Agency for Integrated Care do not track or publish specific success or rejection rates for these applications. Interested parties requiring further assistance should approach the Agency for Integrated Care directly.

results.title: Data on Women in Elective Egg Freezing Programme by Participation Numbers and Age since July 2023, and Average Cost of One Complete Cycle at Public Hospitals
results.summary: Since the elective egg freezing programme launched in June 2023, over 800 women have completed cycles by year-end 2025. The age distribution shows 57% are 35 or older, 39% are aged 30–34, and 4% are under 30. At public hospitals, the average total out-of-pocket cost for one complete cycle is approximately $10,000. These figures reflect participation numbers, demographic breakdowns, and pricing as stated by the Minister for Health.

results.title: Day Activity Centre Clients with Primary Caregivers Aged Above 65
results.summary: Approximately 500 Day Activity Centre clients have at least one primary caregiver aged 65 or older. The Ministry of Social and Family Development pilots community-based alternatives to residential care, including the Enabled Living Programme (launched 2025) and the upcoming Home Support Programme pilot, to support aging in the community. Adult Disability Homes remain a last resort; as of December 2025, over 1,000 persons reside in 11 MSF-funded homes operating above 80% capacity. The Ministry monitors demand with partners to ensure sufficient capacity. Families can access care planning resources via SG Enable's Enabling Guide and tap community support networks like CaringSG for peer guidance.

results.title: Decisions on Opening Hours of Public Libraries
results.summary: The National Library Board (NLB) determines library opening hours based on patron demand, operational sustainability, and mall operating times. Mall-based libraries typically open at 11.00 am due to mall constraints. NLB has piloted self-help services from 9.00 am to 11.00 am at Harbourfront Library and Yishun Library since 2024 and 2025 respectively. The agency plans to roll out similar earlier hours at more operationally feasible locations, including Clementi Library after its revamp. Further details regarding the rollout schedule will be released in due course.

results.title: Dedicated Mental Health Support Services Beyond Helplines for Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) employs a multi-pronged approach for migrant domestic workers (MDWs), integrating upstream touchpoints like the settling-in programme and six-monthly medical examinations with NGO-led support from the Centre for Domestic Employees (CDE) and Brahm Centre. These entities provide 24/7 helplines, counselling, recreational activities, and a peer support network of "Care Sisters" trained in psychological first aid and suicide prevention. While serious cases are escalated for medical treatment, MOM does not collect data on the specific utilisation of these mental health services in the past five years. A 2024 joint study with the Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health and Institute of Mental Health indicates that most MDWs successfully seek help for emotional issues, though a direct needs assessment by the Ministry remains unconfirmed.

results.title: Developments and Considerations for Assessing US Invitation to Join Board of Peace
results.summary: The Singapore Government is currently assessing the United States' invitation for Singapore to join the Board of Peace. Specific developments since October 2025 prompting this formal evaluation have not been detailed in the transcript. The Ministry has not yet stated whether it views the initiative as a shift toward an alternative US-led international order, nor has it confirmed plans to coordinate a unified regional response with ASEAN partners. Additionally, the Government has not outlined specific considerations regarding the invitation or plans to engage Singaporeans in humanitarian efforts for Gaza reconstruction.

results.title: Discrepancies in Immigration Entry and Exit Records
results.summary: According to the Minister for Home Affairs, ICA records currently identify 500 to 600 long-term overstayers, representing less than 0.001% of annual visitor arrivals. The median duration of overstays for arrested foreigners has remained stable since 2019, averaging approximately three months annually. These figures address the specific queries regarding the scale of record discrepancies and trends in overstay lengths. No further details on tracking methodologies or future policy changes were provided in the response.

results.title: Disqualification Criteria for Hiring Live-in Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: Employers may be debarred from hiring migrant domestic workers for serious offences including physical or sexual abuse, non-payment of salary, inadequate food or rest, and instructing unsafe tasks. The debarment period depends on offence severity, with egregious violent offenders facing permanent debarment. The Ministry of Manpower assesses household suitability before lifting restrictions. The Minister confirmed that individuals with criminal convictions for violent offenses against MDWs are prohibited from hiring live-in migrant domestic workers.

results.title: Distribution of Local Employees Retrenchment Benefits from 2023 to 2025
results.summary: From 2020 to 2025, approximately 90% of eligible local employees received retrenchment benefits based on Mandatory Retrenchment Notification data from companies with 10 or more employees. Among these recipients, about 80% received benefits equivalent to at least two weeks' salary per year of service, aligning with Tripartite Advisory norms. The Minister noted that data for 2023 to 2025 specifically is available within this broader reporting period. No survey on retrenchment benefits was conducted as the existing MRN data suffices for the requested metrics.

results.title: Empowering HDB and Town Council Officers to Issue Mediation Orders and Provide Admissible Statements in Areas Where Community Relations Unit Do Not Operate
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that HDB and Town Council officers are already empowered to issue mediation directions for neighbour noise disputes under the Directed Mediation framework, currently piloted in Tampines before expanding to more towns. Non-public officers remain excluded from issuing these directions due to the potential fine of up to $1,500 for non-compliance. Regarding evidence, the Community Disputes Resolution Tribunal may consider any relevant information and can request HDB or Town Council officers to provide statements for its proceedings. The Community Relations Unit retains the authority to appoint these officers, with no new legislative changes proposed for areas outside the unit's operation.

results.title: Ensuring Sufficient Budget Meal Offerings for Those with Genuine Need
results.summary: The Minister for National Development directed Members to refer to a combined reply issued by the Ministry of National Development on 3 February 2026 regarding budget meals in HDB coffee shops. This response addresses questions from Assoc Prof Terence Ho and Ms He Ting Ru concerning the implementation of optional budget meals and ensuring sufficient offerings for those unable to afford regular-priced meals. The specific details of the measures outlined in that combined reply are not provided in this transcript.

results.title: Establishing Government-operated Adult Disability Homes and Day Activity Centres to Complement Social Service Agency-run Ones
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) confirmed it has no plans to establish Government-operated Adult Disability Homes or Day Activity Centres, maintaining a model where appointed Social Service Agencies deliver services under the new Social Residential Homes Act passed in 2025. To address capacity constraints, MSF will add one new Adult Disability Home by 2030 and increase Day Activity Centre capacity by 500 places, raising the total from 1,800 to 2,300 by that year. Urgent cases are prioritized through optimized placements, while SG Enable facilitates referrals to available facilities and pilots community-based alternatives like the Enabled Living Programme to reduce reliance on residential care.

results.title: Expansion Plans for International Schools with Singaporean Curriculum in Southeast Asia
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) confirmed it has no plans to establish new international schools with the Singaporean curriculum in Southeast Asia. For Singaporeans relocating abroad, children may apply for Leave of Absence to access online learning resources while overseas, facilitating a smooth transition back to the local education system upon return. MOE also supports direct readmission to schools and assists with placement into mainstream institutions for students advancing to secondary or junior college levels.

results.title: Expansion of Legal Aid Scheme for Persons With Special Needs Ineligible for Pro Bono Legal Services
results.summary: The Legal Aid Bureau and Public Defender's Office provide aid to citizens and permanent residents with special needs who pass means and merits tests. Applicants with extenuating circumstances failing the means test undergo independent review by the Means Test Panel for possible support. Pro Bono SG offers assistance at Community Law Centres and clinics, including the Ad Hoc Pro Bono Scheme for needy individuals with exceptional circumstances who do not qualify for other schemes. The Minister confirmed these existing mechanisms address the query regarding targeted support for ineligible persons.

results.title: Expansion of and Training for Mental Health Counsellors Workforce
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not actively track mental health counsellor manpower data but notes that voluntary registrations with the Singapore Association for Counselling (SAC) increased from 969 in 2022 to 1,838 in 2025. Social service agencies and public health institutions continuously review service needs and recruit additional counsellors to meet demands, while newly trained practitioners receive varied support including supervision, structured induction, and professional development. Entry-level counsellors must receive clinical supervision from a Registered Counsellor or Registered Clinical Supervisor under SAC's constitution. Training pathways are guided by the National Mental Health Competency Training Framework and the Skills Framework for Social Service, with postgraduate options available through the Singapore University of Social Sciences, National Institute of Education, and College of Allied Educators.

results.title: Expected Improvements to Travel Times, Traffic Resilience and Connectivity with Completion of Loyang Viaduct
results.summary: The Loyang Viaduct, an integral component of the Changi Northern Corridor development, will function as an alternative road corridor to decant traffic and enhance network resilience for the Loyang, Pasir Ris, and Changi regions. Upon completion, motorists and bus commuters traveling from the Tampines Expressway and Loyang Avenue junction to Nicoll Drive during morning peak hours will see average travel times reduced from approximately 22 minutes to 16 minutes, representing a 30% saving. This infrastructure upgrade aims to support industrial, business, and aviation growth in the area.

results.title: Exploitation of Youths Involved in Vaping Offences as Distributors or Intermediaries and Rehabilitation Pathways for First-time Youth Offenders
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated there is no increasing trend of youths being exploited as distributors of e-vaporisers. The Health Sciences Authority assesses cases by examining product quantity, types, and evidence of intent to sell to distinguish users from organised sellers. Courts consider factors such as the offender's age and history of past offences when determining appropriate sentences. No specific diversion or rehabilitation pathways for first-time youth offenders were detailed in the response.

results.title: Extension of SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance for Clinical Mental Health Counselling Training
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed there are no plans to extend the SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance to postgraduate clinical mental health counselling programmes, as the allowance currently supports training up to the undergraduate degree level. While the allowance remains unavailable for postgraduate studies, mid-career Singaporeans may utilise their $4,000 SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career) to offset fees for such programmes offered by Autonomous Universities. The government will continue to monitor the shortage of trained professionals and adjust support levels accordingly.

results.title: Feedback on Bus Captains' Handling of Passengers with Strollers or Prams on Public Buses
results.summary: From 2023 to 2025, the Land Transport Authority received an average of one monthly complaint regarding bus captains' handling of passengers with strollers or prams. New captains receive training at the Singapore Bus Academy on assisting passengers needing special assistance, including those with strollers. These skills are also included in refresher programmes for in-service captains to maintain best practices. LTA has not indicated plans to implement additional specific measures beyond existing training frameworks.

results.title: Five-Year Trend Analysis of Disguised Retrenchment Cases Reported to MOM and TAFEP
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices (TAFEP) received fewer than 10 complaints annually regarding disguised retrenchments. These cases accounted for less than 1% of total complaints received each year. The Ministry and TAFEP have not reported specific figures exceeding this threshold for the specified period. No further details on complaint resolution or policy changes were provided in the transcript.

results.title: Gaps in Support Pathways for Individuals with Serious Mental Health Conditions Who Are Unable to Maintain Community Living
results.summary: The Minister for Health clarified that Singapore operates psychiatric intermediate- to long-term care facilities, including nursing homes, rehabilitation centres, and shelter homes, to support stable individuals with varying severity. These community and step-down care models enable those with severe but stable conditions to progressively reintegrate. The response did not address specific policy gaps for individuals unable to maintain community living who do not meet Institute of Mental Health admission criteria. No new plans or timelines were announced to resolve this specific pathway issue.

results.title: Government Call Verification Measures to Protect Seniors against Scams
results.summary: The Government has assessed that heightened scam vigilance has not yet significantly impacted public service delivery, though it is exploring permanent solutions to help residents verify legitimate calls. A reply detailing these measures was published following the 23 September 2025 sitting. Current advisories warn that officials will never request money transfers, bank log-in details, or unofficial app installations; these warnings are disseminated via websites, social media, and physical letters. The Cyber Security Agency of Singapore and the Ministry of Home Affairs are piloting a National Simulated Scams Exercise focused on government impersonation, with registration available at https://go.gov.sg/NSSE. For immediate verification, the public is directed to the 24/7 ScamShield Helpline at 1799.

results.title: Grant to IMF's Trust for Special Poverty Reduction and Growth Operations for the Heavily Indebted Poor Countries
results.summary: Parliament resolved that the Monetary Authority of Singapore may grant up to 4,480,000 Special Drawing Rights (approximately US$6.1 million) to the International Monetary Fund. This funding supports debt relief efforts for Sudan under the Trust for Special Poverty Reduction and Growth Operations for the Heavily Indebted Poor Countries. The motion was moved pursuant to section 6A(2)(d) of the Bretton Woods Agreements Act 1966 on behalf of the Deputy Prime Minister and Minister-in-charge of MAS.

results.title: Guidelines for Proportion of Work To Be Completed In School and At Home
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) does not dictate specific proportions of schoolwork to be completed in-school versus at home, leaving such decisions to teachers' professional judgement and students' learning needs. While MOE monitors system-level implementation, all schools maintain homework policies that articulate objectives and regulate load, with regular reviews to balance student learning and well-being. For pre-schools, home-based activities like take-home kits are encouraged to foster family bonding and extend learning. Similarly, Institutes of Higher Learning enforce their own academic policies to ensure appropriate student workloads aligned with learning outcomes.

results.title: Headcount Retention and Technology Transfer for Citizen Workforce of Singapore-based AI Company Recently Acquired by Meta
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not intervene in commercial acquisition agreements or disclose specific workforce demographics, including the percentage of Singapore citizens at Manus prior to its acquisition by Meta. While MOM does not guarantee local headcount retention or technology transfer terms, the Economic Development Board (EDB) facilitates skills transfer through initiatives like the Enterprise Compute Initiative (ECI) with partners such as Microsoft. Additionally, companies can access reskilling and upskilling support from Workforce Singapore and SkillsFuture Singapore to equip Singaporeans for new roles. The Government maintains that all companies, whether new, redomiciled, or existing, must comply with Singapore's manpower laws and fair hiring practices without differential treatment.

results.title: Health Insurance Claim Disputes Filed with MAS or FIDReC and Proportion Resolved in Favour of Policyholders
results.summary: Over the 2022–2024 period, FIDReC handled an average of 246 annual insurance liability disputes, with 95 involving health insurance; independent assessors ruled in favour of policyholders in only 4% of these health claims. The Minister explained that the low success rate reflects complex cases where insurers followed policy terms or where non-disclosure of material medical history justified claim exclusions. While only 0.01% of all claims reach FIDReC, the system ensures fairness: MAS requires insurers to prove material non-disclosure before rejecting claims and can mandate reviews or repayments for non-compliance. Consumers should first contact insurers for complaints, escalating to FIDReC mediation or adjudication if unresolved, with over 85% of FIDReC cases settled through these channels.

results.title: High Speed Pursuits Initiated by Police and Safety Protocols for Public Safety
results.summary: The Police do not track the number of high-speed pursuits initiated or resulting in accidents. Safety protocols mandate that only trained officers conduct pursuits, while untrained officers adhere to stricter limits. Officers use sirens and blinkers to alert road users and terminate pursuits if danger escalates. Specific training is provided for high-speed vehicle pursuits. Details regarding tracking methods for fleeing vehicles are not disclosed due to operational reasons. The Ministry has not indicated a review of safety protocols to prioritize public safety in this context.

results.title: Impact of Aircraft Altitude Reduction on Residential and School Areas in Yio Chu Kang and Measures to Mitigate Noise and Safety Risks
results.summary: No aircraft altitude reductions have occurred over Yio Chu Kang in the past six months. The current minimum flight height restriction is approximately 210 metres, mandated by International Civil Aviation Organization requirements to ensure safe operations at Seletar Airport. To mitigate noise, the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore routes aircraft away from residential areas when wind conditions permit and prohibits flights between 10.00 pm and 7.00 am, with exceptions reserved for medical evacuations and emergency flights. The Ministry has not indicated plans to review these existing flight path or altitude limits.

results.title: Impact of Defaults and Fleet Sales on COE Prices and Used-Car Market and Measures to Control Problematic Financing Practices in Coordination with MAS
results.summary: Defaults or fleet sales in the car-leasing sector are unlikely to significantly impact COE prices or the used-car market long-term. Motor vehicle loans from financial institutions must comply with Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) restrictions, while unregulated dealer financing remains under government monitoring. The Government will tighten regulations to curb abusive practices like in-house financing that circumvent loan-to-value limits if necessary. Buyers are advised to secure loans through regulated arrangements to avoid potential risks associated with unregulated financing options.

results.title: Impact of Marginal Bidders in COE Auctions Disproportionately Influencing Clearing Prices across Categories A and B
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that COE bidding operates as a uniform price auction where the clearing price is set at the highest unsuccessful bid plus $1. Data from 2025 indicates that three in four Category A and B COEs were won within 5% of the clearing price, suggesting bidders are price-sensitive. Consequently, the Minister stated that a Pay-As-You-Bid system would not lower prices as all participants pay the clearing price regardless of their bid. In the exercise ending 21 January 2026, car-leasing companies' share of wins dropped to 4% in Category A and 5% in Category B, down from approximately 10% in 2025.

results.title: Impact of US Nutrition Policy Shift Towards Whole Foods to Singapore's Dietary Guidelines and Chronic Disease Management Approach
results.summary: The Minister for Health directed that the question regarding the US nutrition policy shift towards whole foods and its relevance to Singapore's dietary guidelines be answered via a written response. This written answer was provided in Parliament on 3 February 2026. No oral details on specific impacts or policy changes were stated during this session.

results.title: Impact of US' Withdrawal from International Organisations on Singapore and Resultant Contribution Changes
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs stated that the question regarding the US withdrawal from international organisations in January 2026 was addressed in a prior written response to Question No 98 on the Order Paper dated 3 February 2026. No new assessment of impact, changes to Singapore's annual financial contributions, or specific government intentions to deepen investment or adopt a cautious approach were provided in this session. Readers are directed to the earlier response for the relevant policy details.

results.title: Including Input from Singaporeans Who Graduated Overseas in Fresh Graduates Employment Data
results.summary: The fresh graduate employment data published in September 2025 covered only graduates from local autonomous universities and excluded Singaporeans who graduated overseas. Underemployment among residents aged 25 to 29 who graduated overseas was 0.8% in 2025, slightly higher than the 0.7% for local graduates, with overall employment rates for both groups exceeding 90%. While MOM tracks overseas graduate outcomes via the Labour Force Survey and will publish sectoral hiring insights, it does not intend to release separate regular statistics for overseas graduates. Support services, including career matching by Workforce Singapore and NTUC's e2i, remain available to all fresh graduates, including those from overseas universities.

results.title: Increasing Quantum of Benefits and Grants for Large-family Households
results.summary: The Large Families Scheme supports married couples with three or more children, building on existing per-child subsidies for preschool and education. Other schemes like GST vouchers remain means-tested to direct resources to lower-income households rather than solely based on family size. The Government will continue reviewing social schemes to ensure appropriate support reaches those in need. No specific increase to utility rebates or other benefits for large families was confirmed in this response.

results.title: Grant to IMF's Poverty Reduction and Growth Trust
results.summary: The Government approves grants of up to 21 million SDR (approx. US$28.6 million) to the IMF's Poverty Reduction and Growth Trust and 4.48 million SDR (approx. US$6.1 million) to the PRG-HIPC Trust for Sudan's debt relief. These contributions are funded from current resources, do not draw on Past Reserves, and will not reduce domestic spending on healthcare, housing, or education. The Minister clarified that grants remain occasional and case-specific rather than becoming regular or automatic, with additional public communications planned to inform citizens about international aid efforts. Singapore's strategy positions the nation as a force multiplier by hosting multilateral institutions to deliver aid effectively in its own best interests, ensuring funds are used where most needed without triggering the Reserves spending threshold.

results.title: Information for Parents about Preschools' Safety Violations and Enforcement Action Records
results.summary: ECDA does not generally name preschools with safety violations to protect children's identities, particularly when court-imposed gag orders exist. Parents can assess centre quality by checking displayed licence tenure on premises or via LifeSG, where a 24-month licence indicates full compliance while a six-month licence signals areas for improvement. Under the Early Childhood Development Centres Act, operators must immediately notify parents of serious incidents affecting their child and report them to ECDA. Enforcement actions range from licence revocation for severe systemic breaches to regulatory restrictions, with information release contingent on court rulings and public interest. Recent regulatory enhancements include mandatory closed-circuit television installation and updated Code of Practice requirements covering safety, hygiene, and staff deployment.

results.title: Investigation Findings of Accident Involving Autonomous Vehicle on 17 January 2026
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the question regarding the autonomous vehicle accident on 17 January 2026 was already addressed in the reply to Question 59 during the 4 February 2026 Parliament sitting. No new details on the vehicle's response, cause of the accident, or specific operational data were provided in this session. The Minister indicated that the existing parliamentary record contains the requested information, and no further clarification or timeline was announced.

results.title: Issuing Implementation Directive under OCHA to Detect and Remove Moneylending Advertisements on Social Media that Target Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs has not issued an Implementation Directive under the Online Criminal Harms Act for unlicensed moneylending ads targeting migrant domestic workers, citing adequate existing collaboration between the Police and platforms like Meta and TikTok. Between 2023 and 2025, this partnership resulted in the flagging of over 10,900 accounts and advertisements for termination. Implementation Directives are reserved for urgent needs or cases of limited platform cooperation, which the Ministry currently does not identify for this specific offence. Consequently, the approach for impersonation scams has not been extended to illegal moneylending advertisements.

results.title: Licensing Criteria and Approval Processes for Central Kitchens in Shophouse Units
results.summary: Central kitchens are not permitted in shophouse units due to their significant food handling scale and distribution requirements. Instead, they must be located within designated food zones or approved food factories equipped with necessary infrastructure. Operations in these zones face more frequent Singapore Food Agency inspections based on food safety risks and track records. Existing safeguards are deemed sufficient for these high-risk operations, which differ from standard food and beverage establishments.

results.title: Longer-term Support for SMEs to Maintain Employment and Wages of Older Workers
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower confirmed the Senior Employment Credit (SEC) provides wage offsets of up to 7% for Singaporean employees aged 60 and above earning less than $4,000 monthly, applicable to SMEs. This support is extended until end-2026 while a Tripartite Workgroup on Senior Employment reviews the broader strategy to help employers manage multi-generational workforces. The Workgroup is currently studying measures to build capabilities for managing diverse age groups and empowering senior workers to remain productive. Recommendations from this review are expected later this year, with further updates to be provided upon release.

results.title: Managing Public Health and Food Safety Risks with New SAFE Framework
results.summary: The Safety Assurance for Food Establishment (SAFE) framework, implemented in two phases starting 19 January 2026, replaces annual physical decals with real-time digital grading via QR codes on food licences to reflect year-round performance. Establishments are graded A, B, or C based on inspection history; Category 1 entities seeking Grade A must implement a Food Safety Management System and appoint an Advanced Food Hygiene Officer, while those with major lapses or 12 demerit points face automatic downgrading to Grade C. Small businesses receive up to 70% SkillsFuture subsidies for AFHO training and 50% EnterpriseSG support for FSMS certification. SFA maintains a targeted, risk-based inspection regime where high-risk or poor-performing outlets face more frequent checks, with suspensions triggered by court convictions or the Point Demerit System to ensure public safety.

results.title: Managing Teething Issues of Autonomous Vehicle Shuttle Implementation in Punggol
results.summary: Following an AV shuttle accident in Punggol, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) and ComfortDelGro have jointly reviewed operating procedures for transitions between autonomous and manual driving. The Acting Minister for Transport emphasized that this incident underscores the necessity of a familiarisation phase to allow vehicles to adapt to local conditions and for safety officers to understand autonomous vehicle behaviour. LTA will continue collaborating with fleet operators to ensure safe deployment while maintaining the implementation timeline. Members are directed to LTA's 29 January 2026 news release for further details.

results.title: Measures to Ensure Safe, Nutritious and Affordable Food in Schools
results.summary: Questions regarding new safety measures for school meals, recourse for food safety breaches, and support for schools partnering with local businesses were raised. The Minister for Education stated these inquiries were addressed in a response delivered on 3 February 2026 concerning the school canteen and Central Kitchen Meal Model. This response will be published on the Ministry of Education's website. Specific details on new measures, timelines, or eligibility conditions were not provided in this transcript.

results.title: Measures to Protect Workers and Prevent Mass Casualties in High-impact Road Accidents
results.summary: The Ministry regularly reviews worker safety measures on lorries, citing recent implementations like canopy requirements, higher protective side railings, lower speed limits, and minimum rear-deck space standards. Lorries with a laden weight between 3,500 kg and 12,000 kg must install speed limiters by 1 July 2027 to cap maximum speed at 60 km/h, with the Land Transport and Related Matters Bill 2026 introducing heavier penalties for related offences. While the Ministry has consulted on retrofitting seatbelts for rear-deck workers, it notes that retrofitting existing lorries may compromise structural integrity and create new safety risks. Additionally, manufacturers currently refuse to fit seatbelts on new lorries or vouch for their safety due to liability concerns.

results.title: Median Starting Salary for Engineering Graduates Compared with Those in Other Disciplines
results.summary: In 2023, the median starting salary for engineering graduates was $4,400 for autonomous university students and $2,800 for polytechnic students, figures comparable to other disciplines. The Ministry of Education does not track engineering graduates entering non-engineering roles within three years of graduation. Remuneration and career progression are determined by market demand for specific skillsets. Sector agencies will collaborate with industry to build talent pipelines and address manpower needs, while higher learning institutes will ensure curriculum relevance and support upskilling initiatives to align with sector-specific requirements.

results.title: Monitoring Commercial Rental Trends and Tenant Composition in Heritage Conservation Zones, Suburban Towns and Neighbourhood Centres
results.summary: MPs queried whether the Government monitors commercial rental trends and tenant composition in heritage conservation zones like Kampong Glam and suburban towns. They sought clarification on the impact of rising rents on retaining longstanding retailers and preserving cultural character. The Minister for National Development noted that the Urban Redevelopment Authority's Retail Rental Index currently reflects only Central Region leases. A reply to similar questions was issued on 3 February 2026, but specific measures to mitigate displacement of heartland businesses were not detailed in this transcript.

results.title: Monitoring of Areas where Foreign Domestic Workers Congregate
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower monitors migrant domestic worker congregation hotspots on rest days and collaborates with town councils and residents' committees to co-create solutions for noise and littering. Since 2025, the Ministry has piloted accessible community spaces in Clementi and Paya Lebar to provide alternative gathering areas on Sundays, engaging non-governmental organisation partners for recreational activities and training. Information regarding these spaces is shared via MOM's MDW WhatsApp channel. The Ministry plans to expand the number of alternative gathering spaces while recognising that public spaces remain shared by all residents and workers.

results.title: Monitoring of Donees and Deputies Appointed under Mental Capacity Act for Compliance with Fiduciary Duties
results.summary: The Office of the Public Guardian (OPG) monitors donees and deputies under the Mental Capacity Act by requiring annual reports detailing decisions, property management, and affairs handling to assess compliance with Court orders. Suspected abuse triggers investigations, potentially involving a Board of Visitors comprising medical professionals and elder care practitioners to assess the individual's condition and care standards. Non-compliant donees or deputies face closer supervision, while evidence of fraud or financial mismanagement allows OPG to apply to the Court for suspension or revocation of appointments, requiring restitution of losses. Additionally, OPG may refer cases to the police for criminal action. The Minister did not confirm plans for new periodic reporting mandates or specific sanctions beyond existing Court applications and police referrals.

results.title: Number of PMA Users Certified by Registered Medical Professionals
results.summary: Certification for personal mobility aid users under the Active Mobility Act has not yet commenced. Starting 27 February 2026, eligible users will be able to undergo an Assessment for Mobility Scooter conducted by doctors and occupational therapists. The Acting Minister for Transport directed members to the Second Reading of the Land Transport and Related Matters Bill for further details on the programme. No certification data is available for years since 2019 as the process is pending implementation.

results.title: Plans for Adequate Designated Public Fishing Zones
results.summary: The Public Utilities Board does not actively plan for fishing zones but will assess requests for recreational fishing along waterways and reservoirs based on safety considerations and input from relevant land planning agencies. The Minister clarified that there are no current plans to open more designated fishing zones or conduct public consultations specifically for incorporating fishing zones into town planning and design. Consequently, the availability of fishing zones remains subject to individual safety assessments rather than a proactive expansion strategy.

results.title: Plans for Alternative Stadium with Higher Capacity than Jalan Besar Stadium
results.summary: The Ministry confirmed that NS Square and Toa Payoh Integrated Development, with capacities of 30,000 and 10,000 respectively, are under construction and scheduled for operational completion in 2027 and 2030 to provide alternative venues for major international football matches. In the interim, the Ministry employs innovative solutions, such as temporarily adding spectator stands to Bishan Stadium to increase capacity to 10,000 for specific events like the AFC Champions League Two's Final. These measures aim to address scheduling conflicts at the National Stadium and ensure Singapore can host major tournaments.

results.title: Privacy Concerns of Residents of Landed Properties Located Near Towering BTO Projects
results.summary: All Build-to-Order projects are designed per prevailing building codes, including setback guidelines to ensure adequate spacing from adjacent developments. The Housing and Development Board adopts privacy-sensitive designs featuring strategic building orientation and landscaping, such as planting dense trees along common boundaries for visual screening. No new standards or strengthened regulations were announced; existing architectural measures are deemed sufficient to address privacy concerns for residents in landed properties near new BTO projects.

results.title: Programmes or Grants for Media Startups Producing Local Content
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information confirmed that the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) currently supports local media startups, including those producing content featuring local culture, customs, and traditions, through existing programmes focused on content creation and capabilities development. Specific details regarding available grants and eligibility criteria are available on the IMDA website. The Minister did not state whether new programmes or grants would be introduced in response to the query.

results.title: Land Transport and Related Matters Bill
results.summary: The Land Transport and Related Matters Bill introduces new regulations effective 1 June 2026, setting a 6 km/h speed limit for Personal Mobility Aids (PMAs) and decriminalising missed ERP charges as administrative matters. While supporting the Bill's intent to strengthen enforcement and align policy with ground realities, speakers highlighted concerns regarding the affordability and accessibility of the Certificate of Medical Need (COMN) for PMA users, particularly for lower-income households. Clarifications were provided that foreign-registered heavy vehicles must comply with road traffic laws but are exempt from mandatory speed limiters due to differing international speed limits, whereas all lorries operating on Singapore roads will require them. The Bill also addresses technical challenges for OBU installation on older vehicles and outlines enforcement strategies for unsafe devices, ensuring a balance between safety, operational feasibility, and public trust in automated systems.

results.title: Proposal for SkillsFuture to Partner AUs to Provide Subsidised Courses in High-demand Sectors and Implementing Consolidated Lifelong Learning Accounts
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that autonomous universities currently deliver training programmes funded by the Ministry of Education and SkillsFuture Singapore to address upskilling needs across emerging skills, career transitions, and full qualifications. All Singaporeans receive a non-expiring $500 SkillsFuture Credit at age 25, with additional support via the Level-Up Programme and Training Allowance provided at age 40 to facilitate substantive skills reboots. Furthermore, the Career and Skills Passport serves as a personal skills account for every adult to track skill acquisition, while SSG is developing personalised training advisories to guide individuals throughout their working lives. The response did not explicitly confirm the proposal to formally partner with autonomous universities or implement consolidated lifelong learning accounts as requested.

results.title: Proposal to Use Life Expectancy Factor of 83.5 Years When Calculating CPF Savings for Property Purchases
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that CPF usage for property purchases is governed by the Home for Life condition, which requires the remaining lease to cover the youngest owner until at least age 95, rather than the Department of Statistics Singapore's average life expectancy of 83.5 years. This safeguard ensures housing adequacy for seniors who may live beyond the average lifespan. While the Minister acknowledged the concerns of mid-career renters unable to afford private homes or HDB flats due to the 95-year lease requirement, he emphasized that any review must balance immediate housing needs with long-term life tenure. The Ministry of National Development and HDB will consider appropriate ways to assist these individuals while maintaining the core principle of a home for life.

results.title: Publicly Accessible Monitoring Registry Tracking Removal Status of Asbestos from Older Buildings
results.summary: There is no central public registry for asbestos in older buildings; owners must ensure compliance with Workplace Safety and Health (Asbestos) Regulations. Asbestos surveys are mandatory before works in buildings constructed before 1 January 1991. Removal is required only if materials are damaged or will be disturbed during works. Owners must engage MOM-approved contractors for removal. Materials in good condition pose minimal health risks. The Ministry does not maintain a registry tracking removal status or monitor visitor exposure risks directly, leaving operational management to building owners.

results.title: Raising Renewal Age Limit for Migrant Domestic Workers Aged 60 Years and above for Long-term Family Employment
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower maintains the 60-year renewal age limit for migrant domestic workers to balance household demands with rising medical costs. However, on appeal, the Ministry may approve work permit renewals beyond this limit for workers aged 60 and above who have served the same family for 20 years or more. Approval decisions consider the worker's health, experience, and the employer's specific household needs. The current framework does not mandate automatic renewal beyond age 60 but allows for case-by-case exceptions.

results.title: Rate of Commercial Viability of RIE-funded Deep Tech Projects and Implementing Outcome-linked Grant Funding Models
results.summary: The Minister stated that no single metric defines commercial viability for RIE-funded deep tech projects, which require years to mature. Instead, the Government uses diverse indicators including IP licensing, capability building, startup growth, and catalysed private investment. For instance, approximately 80% of RIE2025 corporate laboratories are expected to generate licensing revenue from jointly created IP. While Singapore reviews international models like Israel's, its governance and funding structures remain adapted to local ecosystem needs. Value is captured through licensing revenue and equity returns when startups achieve commercial success, alongside anchoring global and local industry partners to strengthen the economy.

results.title: Rationale for Singapore Citizen Minors' Applications for New or Replacement Passports Without Parental Consent
results.summary: Under the Passports Regulations 2007, parental consent is mandatory for Singapore citizen minors below 16 years old but not required for those aged 16 and above. This age threshold aligns with section 361 of the Penal Code, which criminalizes kidnapping from lawful guardianship only for minors under 16. The regulation balances parental consent requirements with family discretion, allowing 16-year-olds and older to apply for new or replacement passports independently.

results.title: Reasonable Asking Price for Flats in EIP Buyback Scheme
results.summary: The Minister clarified that HDB assesses buyback requests case-by-case for owners facing genuine selling difficulties when EIP quotas are reached. Eligibility requires attempting to sell at a reasonable price for at least six months, with reasonableness determined by comparing marketed prices against recently transacted flats of similar attributes available on the "Check Resale Flat Prices" e-service. For approved cases, HDB appoints a licensed valuer to determine a fair offer based on established valuation principles, allowing owners three months to consider the proposal. The response did not specify whether the final buyback price includes a mandatory EIP-related discount or detail the specific calculation methodology for such adjustments.
