results.title: Reasons for Increase in Lack of Full-time Employment Offers by Polytechnic Graduates
results.summary: Employment fluctuations for polytechnic graduates stem from moderated job vacancies and cautious hiring sentiment amid economic uncertainty, particularly affecting outward-oriented sectors like information and communications. While overall outcomes remain stable with roughly 90% of graduates employed within six months, the information and digital technologies and engineering clusters show marginally higher rates of applicants without full-time offers compared to other courses. The Ministry addresses this through career coaching, employment facilitation, and referrals to Workforce Singapore and NTUC's Employment and Employability Institute. Additionally, sectoral initiatives such as the IMDA's TechSkills Accelerator provide industry-relevant training to enhance graduate employability.

results.title: Protecting Resident Well-being amidst Commercial Activity
results.summary: To balance resident well-being with commercial viability in mixed-use neighbourhoods, the government addresses disamenities like noise and safety through an integrated framework of prevention, governance, and enforcement. Current safeguards are deemed insufficient due to legislative gaps, siloed enforcement, coarse zoning, and weak deterrence, prompting a shift toward practical, predictable measures rather than punitive actions. Since September 2024, HDB has tightened quotas on licensed massage establishments, resulting in the eviction of nearly 40 operators in 2025 following joint raids by SPF and HDB. Additionally, HDB mandates that coffee shops cease using outdoor refreshment areas to mitigate congestion and noise. Agencies including URA, HDB, and SPF collaborate to regulate shop mixes in private and HDB-owned spaces while adhering to existing regulations, ensuring that market forces operate within a structured environment that preserves liveability without stifling enterprise.

results.title: Reference Data for Determining Baby Bonus Quantum
results.summary: The Government does not publish official estimates of child-raising expenditures, citing significant variation based on family needs and choices. Instead, Baby Bonus quantum and cashflow are determined using household income, expenditure data, and parent feedback, resulting in a structure that provides a larger upfront cash gift to address higher initial costs at birth and infancy. Beyond the Baby Bonus, families receive support within the first 36 months through the Child Development Account (CDA) First Step Grant and government co-matching. Additional means-tested subsidies for infant and childcare fees were enhanced in 2020 by raising the income ceiling from $7,500 to $12,000, with further fee cap reductions for Anchor and Partner Operator preschools implemented in 2023 and 2024.

results.title: Regulating Fees Charged by Driving Centres
results.summary: Under the Road Traffic Act, driving schools must charge fees deemed fair and reasonable by the Traffic Police (TP). TP assessed recent fee increase proposals from three driving centres, noting the intent was to cover rising operating costs and raise instructor wages to improve recruitment and retention. Consequently, the driving schools committed to increasing instructor wages, a move expected to increase lesson supply and reduce wait times. The Government has not indicated plans to directly manage learning costs beyond this regulatory framework.

results.title: Regulation for Protection of Consumers Affected by Fraudulent Transactions Involving Third-party Payment Platforms
results.summary: Existing regulatory guidelines, including the E-Payments User Protection Guidelines, define financial institution duties for losses from unauthorised transactions involving third-party payment platforms. Key operational measures include a 12-hour cooling-off period after digital token activation or login to a new device, alongside real-time transaction notification alerts. When fraud involves multiple institutions, customers must report promptly; the Monetary Authority of Singapore expects each institution to investigate its obligations and customer responsibility without disclaiming liability solely due to another party's involvement. Disputes may be escalated to the Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre (FIDReC) for independent mediation and adjudication if customers disagree with an institution's investigation outcome.

results.title: Regulatory Measures against Systematic Base Fare Cuts by Platform Companies after Implementation of CPF Contribution Scheme
results.summary: Between January and September 2025, the monthly median change in net take-home earnings for platform workers opting into the CPF contribution scheme ranged from a 12.7% decrease to an 8.2% increase, with no consistent trend observed due to fluctuations in hours worked and demand-supply seasonality. The Ministry of Manpower reported no evidence of systematic fare reductions by platform operators to offset contribution shares. In September 2025, the Platform Workers Trilateral Group released industry-wide principles establishing baseline expectations for fair, transparent earnings and visibility on structural incentive changes. Platform workers may seek assistance from registered Platform Work Associations regarding concerns about fare and incentive setting practices.

results.title: Regulatory Requirements for Commercial Landlords and Enforcement Measures for Premises Conducting Vice Activities
results.summary: Landlords of commercial properties must exercise due diligence to ensure premises comply with laws and are not used for vice activities. Under section 148(4) of the Women's Charter, property owners face criminal liability if their premises are used for such activities, unless they can prove they had no knowledge and could not have ascertained the misuse through reasonable due diligence at the time of entering tenancy agreements. Police will take enforcement action where warranted.

results.title: Remaining Vacancies in Secondary Schools following DSA Exercise and Affiliated Admissions
results.summary: Since 2019, affiliated secondary schools must reserve at least 20% of their Secondary 1 cohort for non-affiliated students across each posting group. While Direct School Admission (DSA) offers do not reduce total school capacity, DSA admits for non-affiliated students count against this reserved quota. Over the past five years, non-affiliated students comprised roughly half of affiliated cohorts, with only two instances where all reserved places were filled via DSA in a single posting group. The Ministry will study a suggestion to publish DSA offer numbers, previous year's affiliated admissions, and incoming cohort sizes to aid parental decision-making, pending an assessment of whether such historical data has low variance to predict future outcomes effectively.

results.title: Response to Peak-Hour Congestion at Clementi MRT Station Link Bridge and Future Cross-Island Line Interchange Capacity Planning
results.summary: LTA has deployed service ambassadors during peak hours to guide commuters at Clementi MRT station's link bridge to Clementi Mall, following the construction of two additional entrances and bridges in 2015. As part of the Cross Island Line project, the Land Transport Authority plans to replace the existing link bridge with a new, wider structure to accommodate anticipated higher footfall from the future interchange. No specific timeline for this infrastructure replacement was stated in the response.

results.title: Review of IPS Study's Findings on Public Messaging Regarding Citizens' Versus Foreigners' Contributions to Economic Growth
results.summary: The Ministry for Digital Development and Information confirmed that the January 2026 Institute of Policy Studies (IPS) study on public messaging regarding citizens' versus foreigners' contributions was initiated to trial new engagement modalities. The Government agrees that Singaporeans' contributions must be appropriately recognised and currently highlights these achievements through national campaigns, the SG60 celebrations, the "Together in the Lorongs" digital series, the National Day Awards, and the Pioneer and Merdeka Generation packages. While the study's specific insights were not detailed in the response, the Minister stated the Government always aims to do better and welcomes suggestions from members and the public to refine communication strategies. No specific timeline for a formal review of communication strategies was provided.

results.title: Safety Measures and Systems for Bus Captains during Route Diversions and Navigation Support Tools
results.summary: Bus captains receive real-time guidance via voice communication with the Operations Control Centre and pre-journey briefings including route maps during diversions. The Land Transport Authority will collaborate with operators to evaluate and deploy technological solutions, such as mobile navigation apps already used by SBS Transit for bridging operations during rail disruptions. Enhanced tools like real-time GPS prompts remain under evaluation for broader deployment to support captains when route familiarity is disrupted.

results.title: Simplifying Administrative and Reporting Processes for Deputies Appointed under Mental Capacity Act
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development confirmed that deputies appointed under the Mental Capacity Act must submit annual reports to the Office of the Public Guardian (OPG) to safeguard against abuse, though MSF is implementing measures like online submission and finance trackers to reduce burden. While acknowledging feedback that the application process remains daunting for families with limited resources or English proficiency, and that banking constraints persist, the Ministry stated it will continuously review and improve both application and reporting procedures. The Minister also noted the Ministry will consider suggestions to establish an inter-agency work group to coordinate support and explore setting financial thresholds for statutory declarations to handle smaller sums without full deputyship.

results.title: Siting of Cross Island Line's Loyang MRT Station Away from High-density Residential Areas
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the Loyang Cross Island Line (CRL) station location was determined primarily to maximize mid-to-long-term commuter coverage based on existing and future land use plans, while also considering engineering feasibility and land acquisition constraints. To enhance first- and last-mile connectivity for residents in Flora, Toh, and Mariam estates, the Ministry will explore improvements to covered linkways, cycling paths, and bus services, including connections to the upcoming CR4 station. These measures aim to improve access to the new CR3 station without compromising the primary objective of serving the broader catchment area.

results.title: Statistics on Motorists Booked for Failing to Keep Left and Plans on Intensifying Public Education and Enforcement
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2025, the Traffic Police issued an average of 1,930 annual summonses for failing to keep left. The agency will continue enforcing this offence and educating motorists on lane discipline to improve road efficiency and address road hogging. These efforts target heavy, logistics, and slower vehicles occupying leftmost lanes when available, despite existing speed limiter regulations. No specific timeline for intensified enforcement or new educational campaigns was stated beyond the commitment to continue current measures.

results.title: Statistics on School Gastroenteritis Cases by Food Source from 2022 to 2025
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the requested statistics on school gastroenteritis cases by food source from 2022 to 2025 were not provided in the current transcript. Instead, the Minister directed the inquiry to the Ministry of Education's oral reply to Questions 14 to 20, delivered on 3 February 2026. The specific breakdown for central kitchen caterers, school canteens, and general food and beverage outlets remains unaddressed in this session.

results.title: Status of Quantitative Noise Thresholds Review for Residential Boundary Noise
results.summary: The review on quantitative noise thresholds for residential boundary noise was completed in May 2024, with a response provided in October 2024. The National Environment Agency's industrial noise standards are explicitly for different purposes and do not apply to neighbour noise disputes. Further details are available on the Municipal Services Office website and the Second Reading of the Community Disputes Resolution (Amendment) Bill 2024. The Minister did not confirm whether World Health Organization guidelines or specific foreign country thresholds were considered during the review.

results.title: Streamlined Neighbour Dispute Resolution Outlets for Failed Community Mediation Cases
results.summary: The Minister clarified that while empowering the Community Mediation Centre to refer failed mediation cases to the Community Disputes Resolution Tribunals (CDRT) was considered, it remains unimplemented due to confidentiality constraints and potential reluctance among parties to engage in frank discussions. Under the Community Disputes Resolution (Amendment) Act 2024, the Community Relations Unit (CRU) is instead empowered to investigate cases, collect evidence, and submit them to the CDRT, with a pilot currently running in Tampines Town for serious noise and hoarding cases. An enhanced process is being developed to allow the CRU Director-General to apply for CDRT hearings. The CDRT maintains low filing fees, offers fee waivers for those in financial need, and utilizes the Community Justice and Tribunals System (CJTS) for simplified online filing without legal representation.

results.title: Strengthening Legislation on Vapes by Extending Classification of Etomidate and Related Analogues as Class C Controlled Drugs
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs will extend the listing of etomidate and its analogues as Class C controlled drugs under the Misuse of Drugs Act pending new legislation. The Ministry of Health is finalising proposed amendments to strengthen existing vape regulations and will table the Bill in Parliament within the first half of 2026. This interim measure ensures continued control of etomidate until the new legislative framework comes into force.

results.title: Strengthening Monitoring Systems to Prevent Abuse of Prescription Drugs
results.summary: The Ministry of Health and Health Sciences Authority (HSA) oversee registered prescription drugs, requiring HSA approval before legal supply and conducting regular audits on licensed importers and distributors. Multi-agency enforcement operations involving the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA), Central Narcotics Bureau, and Singapore Police Force (SPF) collaborate with HSA to curb illegal import, manufacture, and supply of unregistered health products and illicit cough syrups. Information sharing between SPF, ICA, and HSA strengthens investigations, with detected cases referred for subsequent action. Public education efforts are conducted through selective publicity of these operations to enhance deterrence. The Ministry of Home Affairs explicitly stated it is not involved in providing rehabilitation and support services for prescription drug addicts, focusing instead on supporting abusers of controlled drugs.

results.title: Study on Labour Impacted by AI in SMEs and Plans to Support Workers Substituted by AI
results.summary: The Government is studying AI's impact on Singapore's labour market, noting that effects depend more on business activities and job roles than firm size. While SMEs and MNCs face different contexts, workforce transformation programmes support all firms to redesign jobs and reskill workers alongside AI adoption. Partners tailor support levels, offering closer guidance for larger, complex projects. For workers displaced by AI, the Government will collaborate with companies to facilitate transitions into new roles through reskilling programmes, ensuring all workers remain relevant regardless of their current job or firm type.

results.title: Studying Correlation between Lower-income Households and Diet-related Chronic Diseases
results.summary: The Ministry of Health acknowledges that lower-income households face higher risks of diet-related chronic diseases. To address this, the government prioritizes universal access to healthcare, education, and housing as key preventive measures. Specific initiatives include the Healthier Dining Programme, which provides healthier meals at hawker centres, coffee shops, and food courts, and the Healthier Choice Symbol Programme, which collaborates with supermarkets to increase affordable, healthier house brand products. These efforts aim to improve access to nutritious food options for all Singaporeans, particularly those aged 40 and above.

results.title: Subsidies Available for Knee Osteoarthritis Injectable Therapy and Approval Thresholds for Other Regenerative Injections
results.summary: Currently, only intra-articular corticosteroid injections are subsidised for knee osteoarthritis treatment, while hyaluronic acid and other regenerative injections remain unsubsidised due to insufficient evidence of clinical effectiveness. The Ministry of Health aligns this stance with high-quality international guidelines, noting that subsidies are granted only to treatments assessed as clinically effective, safe, and cost-effective. The Ministry will continue evaluating emerging local and overseas study evidence to reassess eligibility for other regenerative injections as data becomes available.

results.title: Successful Applicants of Phase 2B with No Prior School Affiliation and Proportion of Children of Alumni
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, schools conducting Phase 2B balloting admitted an average of one-third of their Primary One cohort via Phase 1, one-third via Phase 2A, and the remaining one-third via Phases 2B and 2C. The Ministry of Education noted that the Primary One Registration Framework balances factors including sibling convenience, parental ties, proximity, and access for unaffiliated families. While the Minister did not confirm a specific reservation of 40 places for unaffiliated families under Phase 2B, the Ministry is currently reviewing the framework to enhance access for students of different backgrounds.

results.title: Sufficiency of Current Cooperation with Neighbouring Countries to Safeguard Network of Submarine Cables
results.summary: The Infocomm Media Development Authority chairs the ASEAN Working Group on Submarine Cables, which endorsed the Enhanced ASEAN Guidelines for Strengthening Resilience and Repair of Submarine Cables at the ASEAN Digital Ministers' Meeting in January 2026. These guidelines establish best practices for member states to protect cables and expedite repairs. The Ministry of Digital Development and Information will continue regional cooperation to build capabilities while exploring alternative connectivity, including satellite systems, to enhance digital resilience.

results.title: Summonses Issued to Individuals Who Leave Refuse Bags at HDB Void Decks Next to Refuse Bins
results.summary: Under the Environmental Public Health Act 1987, leaving refuse bags beside litter bins at HDB void decks is an offence enforced by the National Environment Agency (NEA). NEA does not track specific enforcement numbers for this scenario but collaborates with Town Councils to educate residents and deploy surveillance cameras for evidence gathering. Persistent cases are addressed through joint efforts with Town Councils to manage cleaning and facilitate investigations. The Minister clarified that enforcement responsibility lies with NEA, not Town Councils, though they work together on education and camera usage.

results.title: Ten-Year Statistics on Employment Pass Exception Approvals and Assessment Criteria
results.summary: From 2016 to 2025, the Employment Pass qualifying salary rose from $3,300 to $5,600, while total issuances increased from 192,300 to 202,100. The Ministry of Manpower grants flexibility on qualifying salary thresholds based on national priorities, supported by collaboration with the Ministry of Trade and Industry and economic agencies. These targeted measures aim to foster economic growth, transformation, and job creation for locals, particularly in attracting new investments and supporting research and innovation. Specific industry breakdowns for non-compliant applications and detailed factors for flexibility are provided in Table 1 of the official response.

results.title: Timeline Status for Multi-Agency Task Force on Invisible Disabilities in Justice System
results.summary: The Inclusive Justice Taskforce was formally launched on 8 January 2026 to support persons with invisible disabilities and mental health conditions within Singapore's criminal justice system. Co-led by Senior Parliamentary Secretary for Law Mr Eric Chua and Ms Peggy Yee, the multi-disciplinary group includes representatives from the private and public sectors, such as lawyers, law enforcement officers, and healthcare experts. The taskforce will examine how society can better support individuals at risk of offending or interacting with the system, including accused persons, victims, and witnesses. A thorough consultative review process will be undertaken, with progress updates and recommendations to be provided in due course as part of the Ministry's ongoing efforts to strengthen legal accessibility and inclusivity.

results.title: Tracking Food and Beverage Outlet Closures and Common Reasons for Closures
results.summary: The Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority (ACRA) tracks all business entity formations and cessations but does not collect granular data distinguishing standalone outlets, local chains, or foreign chains. Between 2015 and 2025, the food and beverage sector saw a net 42% increase in entities, with formations outpacing closures annually. While the Government does not currently track closure reasons or disproportionate impacts on independent operators, it has considered suggestions to collect such data. To support heritage and independent businesses, Enterprise Singapore offers programmes like Food X and Process Optimisation, alongside SME Centres and trade association collaborations aimed at enhancing competitiveness and operational efficiency.

results.title: Tracking of Food Insecurity Prevalence in Singapore
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) does not systematically track the prevalence of food insecurity in Singapore using metrics like the Food Insecurity Experience Scale. While global data indicates Singapore's levels are comparable to high-income economies with a recent increase, the Ministry prioritizes ensuring financial support availability through ComCare assistance at Social Service Offices. MSF regularly reviews measurement ranges to better identify needs and has stated it will consider including food security as a possible area for more systematic measurement in the future. Current efforts rely on ComCare, food charities, and grassroots organisations to provide support to households in need.

results.title: Trend of SingPost's Compliance with Postal Quality of Service Standards and Feedback Received on Service Improvements
results.summary: SingPost has consistently met Postal Quality of Service (QoS) standards set by the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) over the past three years, with 2025 feedback focusing on misdelivered letters and delivery delays. IMDA conducts quarterly simulated letter tests using external independent assessors to verify compliance, publishing results on its website. While Singapore's QoS requirements are among the most stringent globally, with typical delivery times of one to three days compared to two to five days elsewhere, the Ministry will investigate a specific Woodgrove incident involving missing Edusave ceremony invitations. IMDA will review this particular case to ensure continuous service improvement.

results.title: Unsubstantiated Complaints Lodged against Healthcare Workers in Last Three Years and Enforcement Measures to Deter Repeat Offenders
results.summary: Over the last three years, 32% of complaints against healthcare workers were dismissed as unsubstantiated by Professional Boards. While anonymous complaints are investigated, the Ministry does not track specific counts of frivolous or vexatious submissions. Investigations against complainants are not initiated for unsubstantiated cases, though abusive behavior leads to disengagement. To deter false claims, individuals must make a statutory declaration when lodging complaints against registered practitioners. Action may be taken against those who make false declarations, but no specific enforcement measures against repeat complainants were confirmed.

results.title: Update on Development of Potential Dengue Vaccine and Patient Support Service for Dengvaxia Vaccine
results.summary: Dengvaxia remains the sole licensed dengue vaccine in Singapore, available only to those who have already started the three-dose series as the supplier has discontinued it due to low global demand. Two other vaccines, Qdenga and Butantan-Dengue Vaccine, are not approved for use in Singapore, with one application withdrawn and another not submitted. MSD's V181 candidate is currently in Phase 3 clinical trials. The Ministry of Health and relevant agencies will continue monitoring vaccine and therapeutic development as part of a multi-pronged approach to dengue control.

results.title: Update on Hawker Centres and Food Retail Establishments with Smoking Corners
results.summary: The National Environment Agency ceased accepting new smoking corner applications in retail food establishments in June 2017. Existing corners are phased out upon licence termination or during repairs, with numbers dropping from 1,000 in June 2017 to approximately 10 in hawker centres and 270 in retail establishments by December 2025. The Ministry has no current plans to mandate removal as a licence renewal condition. Stakeholders are engaged progressively for removal when opportune.

results.title: Update on Inclusive Support Programme Pilot
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) will progressively add approximately 600 Inclusive Support Programme (InSP) places starting in 2026 for children requiring medium-level early intervention in preschool settings. Evaluation results indicate significant developmental progress for participating children and enhanced peer acceptance within inclusive environments. ECDA guidelines permit specific environmental modifications, such as frequency modulation systems for deaf children, with funding available to InSP preschools. Additionally, families can access the Ministry of Social and Family Development's Assistive Technology Fund for up to a 90% means-tested subsidy on assistive devices or utilize Child Development Account funds for approved purchases and maintenance.

results.title: Update on Negotiations with US on Tariffs and Economic Restrictions
results.summary: Singapore's exports to the US face a 10% reciprocal tariff rate, alongside ongoing Section 232 investigations into semiconductors and pharmaceuticals which remain exempt from this rate. The Ministry is currently in discussion with US counterparts regarding these tariffs while engaging industry stakeholders to assess potential impacts. No specific timeline for resolution or new policy measures has been announced, and an update will be provided when ready.

results.title: Update on Strengthening Regulation Measures and Legislation on Vape Laws and Etomidate
results.summary: The Ministry of Health is finalising proposed legislative amendments to strengthen regulation of vapes, including those containing harmful substances like etomidate. These amendments will be tabled in Parliament within the first half of 2026. The update addresses the timing for enhanced legal measures targeting vape products with more harmful substances.

results.title: Usage Statistics of AEDs in HDB Towns and Plans for Expansion to Private Developments
results.summary: The Save-a-Life initiative, launched in August 2015, deployed over 5,000 automated external defibrillators (AEDs) in HDB blocks by end-2025, with more than 4,500 recorded uses. The Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) partners with community networks to install AEDs in private residential developments, including landed estates and condominiums. Additionally, SCDF collaborates with the Singapore Heart Foundation to maintain a national registry for publicly accessible AEDs and engages businesses to install devices on their premises.

results.title: Usage of Blue and Orange CHAS Card for Discounted Products and Services at Non-healthcare Retailers
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not collect data on the number of non-healthcare retailers using CHAS Blue and Orange cards for discounts, as these arrangements are private initiatives unrelated to government healthcare subsidies. While some retailers voluntarily offer special benefits to targeted customer segments using these cards, the Government has not conducted a study on the scale or sectors involved. Consequently, there is no official record of the extent of this usage outside the healthcare sector.

results.title: Waiving MOP for HDB Flat Owners Seeking to Sell in View of Neighbour Disputes and Measures to Ensure Fairness and Consistency
results.summary: HDB assesses Minimum Occupation Period (MOP) waiver appeals on a case-by-case basis, prioritizing mediation to resolve neighbour disputes before considering waivers. Most noise complaints are settled through direct resolution or Community Mediation Centre mediation, with Community Disputes Resolution Tribunals serving as a last resort. The Minister stated that specific criteria for waiving MOP due to neighbour disputes are not explicitly defined but are evaluated based on the unique circumstances of each family. No fixed timeline or automatic eligibility threshold for such waivers was provided in the response.
